summary: The core mechanism of TTN involves postnatal failure to clear amniotic fluid secondary to insufficient catecholamines during delivery—this process does not involve heritable changes in beta-adrenergic function. No current data support an association between common variations in ADRB receptors and increased incidence or worse outcomes in infants diagnosed with TTN.
